**Attestation**  
A formal process in {{< var vm.product >}} that enables model owners to document and certify model attributes at specific points in time, supporting compliance and governance processes. The attestation feature takes snapshots of models and their findings, ensuring that changes made after attestation creation do not affect the historical record. This process creates an audit trail of model information and verifies that key controls, governance processes, and documentation requirements have been followed throughout a model's lifecycle.

**Attestation period**  
A defined timeframe during which an attestation process is active, with specific start and end dates. Each period creates a snapshot of model data that remains unchanged throughout the attestation process. These periods are typically scheduled (e.g., "Q1 attestation") and can be configured to align with regulatory or internal review cycles.

**Attestation questionnaire**  
A structured set of questions and checkboxes that model owners must complete as part of the attestation process, documenting their verification of model attributes and compliance requirements.

**Model snapshot**  
A point-in-time capture of model data, including core fields, custom fields, and associated findings, that remains fixed throughout the attestation process even if live data changes. This ensures the historical accuracy of the attestation record.
